decisions.decision: Create a shared <DataTable> React component in sacco-ui/src/components/DataTable.tsx with companion DataTable.css
decisions.rationale: All 16 tables across the app use ledger-table class with duplicated thead/tbody/colgroup markup. A shared component eliminates repetition and ensures consistent UX (virtualization, sticky headers, scroll containers) across all pages.

decisions.decision: Column-definition driven API: DataTable<T> receives columns: ColumnDef<T>[], data: T[], with render functions per column for cell content
decisions.rationale: Pages use diverse cell patterns (badges, stacked text, action buttons, currency amounts, truncated IDs). Render functions keep the component presentational while allowing full customization per page.

decisions.decision: DataTable is presentational only — parent component owns data fetching, filter state, and pagination logic
decisions.rationale: Each page has different filter controls (date ranges, custom Select dropdowns, text search). Coupling data fetching into DataTable would create an overly complex god-component. Filters stay in the page; DataTable just renders rows.

decisions.decision: Virtualization is always on via @tanstack/react-virtual useVirtualizer
decisions.rationale: User chose 'All data tables' for virtualization scope. The virtualizer has negligible overhead for small datasets and significant benefit for large ones. Always-on simplifies the API.

decisions.decision: Optional features via props: expandableRows (with renderExpanded callback), stickyTotals (footer row outside scroll), sortable columns (via onSort callback), loading/skeleton state, empty state message
decisions.rationale: Only Ledger uses expandable rows. Only Ledger and LedgerStatements use totals footer. 6 pages have sort headers. Making these opt-in keeps the component lean for simple tables.

decisions.decision: Column widths defined as CSS custom properties on wrapper element, referenced by both colgroup and any sticky footer grid
decisions.rationale: Pattern proven in Ledger.css — single source of truth for column widths between table layout and grid footer. Prevents width drift.

decisions.decision: SkeletonTableRows remains the loading skeleton, rendered inside tbody when loading=true prop is set
decisions.rationale: Already used by 9 pages. DataTable will render it automatically based on a loading boolean prop.

decisions.decision: Alternating row striping via a getRowClassName callback prop, not automatic
decisions.rationale: Ledger alternates by journal entry (groups of rows), not by individual row index. Other pages may want simple odd/even. A callback gives control to the parent.

decisions.decision: Scroll container height defaults to calc(100vh - 260px), overridable via maxHeight prop
decisions.rationale: Most pages need the table to fill remaining viewport. 260px accounts for header + filter bar + page summary. Pages with different layouts can override.

decisions.decision: Sticky thead with position: sticky, top: 0, z-index: 10 — always enabled
decisions.rationale: No table currently has sticky headers except Ledger. All data tables benefit from persistent column headers during scroll.

decisions.decision: Table frame wrapper with border, border-radius, overflow: hidden — always rendered by DataTable
decisions.rationale: Provides clean visual containment. Matches the Ledger pattern and gives a consistent card-like appearance across all tables.

decisions.decision: Migrate all 16 tables incrementally — start with high-traffic pages (Members, Contributions, Loans) then remaining pages
decisions.rationale: Incremental migration validates the API against real page diversity. High-traffic pages provide the best signal for API fitness.

constraints: Must not break existing page-specific CSS — DataTable CSS should be additive, not override page styles
DataTable must support table-layout: fixed with colgroup for precise column control
The component must be generic: DataTable<T> with TypeScript generics for row type
No data fetching or filter logic inside DataTable — strictly presentational
Virtualization via @tanstack/react-virtual useVirtualizer — no other virtualization library
Must support aria attributes: role, aria-live on totals, aria-hidden on spacer rows
Mobile responsive: horizontal scroll wrapper at 768px breakpoint (existing pattern from components.css)
Keep cursor-based pagination (Load More button) outside DataTable — parent controls it
